learning_outcomes.description: Aural and Practical Skills Musicianship - Demonstrate fluency and command of a set repertoire for this course.
Aural and Practical Skills Musicianship - Analyse and communicate the pitch and rhythmic structures of selected repertoire
Aural and Practical Skills Musicianship - Sing and notate familiar and unfamiliar musical material
Aural and Practical Skills Musicianship - Demonstrate accuracy, confidence and fluency in all tasks
Aural and Practical Skills Musicianship - Demonstrate the ability to work creatively on all tasks
Aural and Practical Skills Musicianship - Engage critically and reflectively with methodologies used for aural studies
Harmony and Keyboard Musicianship - Understand the principles of basic diatonic harmony and counterpoint.
Harmony and Keyboard Musicianship - Recognise in written form and play at the keyboard any type of interval or triad.
Harmony and Keyboard Musicianship - Develop skills in part writing at the keyboard and in written form.
Harmony and Keyboard Musicianship – Analyse short chordal progressions using Roman Numerals or Figured Bass notation.
Harmony and Keyboard Musicianship - Complete a short four-part (SATB) piece utilising the harmonic resources presented in class.

assessments.task_description:
A sequence of two assignments on aural and practical material that is covered in the weekly aural and practical musicianship classes. Information and task sheets for each Practical Task will be made available on Blackboard and discussed in class prior to the submission date. Each Practical Task is worth 15% of the final grade (30% total).

PRACTICAL TASK 1 DUE 4/09/26 5:00 pm

Students will be required to:

- Perform a given 4 bar simple time rhythm while keeping beat and pulse.

- Sing Doh pentatonic, Major and Natural minor scales in tune with ostinato.

- Sing and play one of the given canons at the keyboard.

- Sing a different canon in a group of three with one voice on a part.

- Sing a piece of repertoire from the course while accompanying yourself at the keyboard. Chords in right hand, bass line in left hand.

PRACTICAL TASK 2 Due 9/10/26

Students will be required to:

- Perform a given 4 bar compound time rhythm while keeping beat and pulse.

- Sing all pentatonic and diatonic scales in tune from the same starting note while performing an ostinato.

- Sing and play one of the given canons at the keyboard.

- Sing another canon in a group of three with one voice per part.

- Sing a piece of repertoire from the course accompanying oneself at the keyboard with triads in the right hand and bass notes in the left hand.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Assessment should be submitted according to the instructions provided in class and on the Blackboard site for this course.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 1 grade for each 24 hour period from time submission is due will apply for up to 7 days. After 7 days you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
This assignment covers written (harmony) and keyboard-based materials in the week 1-6 lectures and musicianship keyboard lab classes. An Information and task sheet will be made available on Blackboard and discussed in class prior to the submission date.

The assignment will require both Keyboard and Written (Harmony) responses. Weighting: 20% of the final grade.

The assignment will be available for download and completion within a certain timeframe to be advised by course staff, with announcements on the Blackboard site for this course. 


This Assignment will be assessed on the following criteria:

1. Overall fluency: competence in general ability to use music theory and notation conventions to convey accurate information.

2. Topic-specific knowledge and application: ability to understand, use and write specific elements of music theory and notation in written and keyboard responses.

The results achieved will constitute an overall assessment of the assignment and individual questions will not carry specific grade values. Please also check the rubric that will be available through the Blackboard Grade Centre.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit your written (harmony) responses as a single PDF file and submit your keyboard responses as a single video file.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
- Rhythmic Dictation in simple time

- Rhythmic dictation in compound time

- Interval dictation

- Melodic dictation in minor

- Two-part melodic dictation in major

- Harmonic dictation
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
The Final Exam will require written Written (Harmony) responses only on material covered in Weeks 7-13. 

Weighting: 30% of the final grade.

An information & task sheet will be made available on Blackboard about question types and requirements.

Criteria:

  1. Established Knowledge: assumed competence in all previously covered specific elements and general principles of music theory and notation.
  2. Overall fluency: competence in general ability to use music theory and notation conventions to convey accurate information.
  3. Topic-specific knowledge and application: ability to understand, use and write specific elements of music theory and notation.

The results achieved will constitute an overall assessment of the assignment and individual questions will not carry specific grade values. Please also check the rubric that will be available through the Blackboard Grade Centre.
assessments.submission_guidelines: In-person exam during the central examination period.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.
